patch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline focused on making your brand and its content more likely to appear, and be cited, in the answers provided by AI models, such as ChatGPT and Gemini.
patch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline focused on making your brand and its content more likely to appear, and be cited, in the answers provided by AI models, such as ChatGPT and Gemini.
patch.after: AEO is how you get your brand quoted when someone asks ChatGPT or Gemini a question instead of Googling it.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Human voice vs committee" scored 1/10: Reads entirely like templated marketing copy with no personality.

instructions.evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 1/10 — Every claim is a safe, hedged generality with no stance.

patch.target: AEO has rapidly become crucial for brands aiming for high visibility in AI-powered search and answer experiences.
patch.before: AEO has rapidly become crucial for brands aiming for high visibility in AI-powered search and answer experiences.
patch.after: Most AEO advice is repackaged SEO; the only tactic I've seen actually move citations is publishing original, numbered statistics.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 1/10: Every claim is a safe, hedged generality with no stance.

instructions.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 1/10 — Language is stock phrases: 'path to success', 'stay ahead', 'data-driven'.

patch.target: The path to success is data-driven: structure your content for citation, embed authoritative statistics, use schema.org markup, and track your brand’s performance in LLM-generated answers to stay ahead.
patch.before: The path to success is data-driven: structure your content for citation, embed authoritative statistics, use schema.org markup, and track your brand’s performance in LLM-generated answers to stay ahead.
patch.after: Getting cited is less about 'optimization' and more about being the only page with a number worth stealing.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 1/10: Language is stock phrases: 'path to success', 'stay ahead', 'data-driven'.

instructions.evidence: Point of view vs neutral survey 2/10 — Presents both sides of every question without committing.

patch.target: While AEO and SEO share overlapping tactics, AEO is not just an extension, it requires separate monitoring and technical strategies to win citations in AI-generated answers.
patch.before:
t;text": "While AEO and SEO share overlapping tactics, AEO is not just an extension, it requires separate monitoring and technical strategies to win citations in AI-generated answers."
          }
patch.after:

          }
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs neutral survey" scored 2/10: Presents both sides of every question without committing. (before extended with context for unique match)

instructions.evidence: Quotable sentence 1/10 — Nothing memorable; all sentences are interchangeable definitions.

patch.after: If your page doesn't contain a number a model can steal, it won't get cited—period.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentence" scored 1/10: Nothing memorable; all sentences are interchangeable definitions.

patch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline focused on making your brand and its content more likely to appear, and be cited, in the answers provided by AI models, such as ChatGPT and Gemini. Unlike classic SEO, AEO aims to win visibility in generative answer engines by aligning with citation, quotation, and schema signals that these systems value most. Industry research confirms that AEO has become essential for any brand seeking discoverability in 2026's search landscape.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Unlike classic SEO, AEO aims to win visibility in generative answer engines by aligning with citatio..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (4.05/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 2/5

patch.before: AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Gemini use signals such as structured data, direct quotations, and domain authority to determine which sources to cite. During browsing, these models extract verifiable facts, statistics, and explicit definitions, heavily weighing schema.org markup and sources with clear helpful content signals. OpenAI documents that citations are more likely when content is directly relevant, quoted, and uses structured data accepted by the LLM.[openai-cite][geo-arxiv][schema-org-defined-term]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "During browsing, these models extract verifiable facts, statistics, and explicit definitions, heavil..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (4.05/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 3/5

patch.before: LLMs favor structured, easily extractable elements such as schema.org markup for definitions, statistics, and quotations. In-depth guidance on the Helpful Content System by Google points out that E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) and people-first content are foundational. Adding DefinedTerm schema increases the likelihood of your definitions being used in generative answers.[schema-org-defined-term][google-search-central]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "LLMs favor structured, easily extractable elements such as schema.org markup for definitions, statis..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (4.05/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 4/5

patch.before: The most influential answer engines are ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, all of which rely on LLMs to generate answers citing external sources.[openai-cite][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "The most influential answer engines are ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, all of which rely o..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (4.05/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 5/5

patch.before: Use monitoring tools to track citations, mentions, and sentiment across generative search engines and compare results over time.[semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Use monitoring tools to track citations, mentions, and sentiment across generative search engines an..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: Specific citations (names/prices/dates) 4/10 — Cites a few engines and one arXiv paper, but no prices, people, dates, or concrete examples.

patch.target: Industry analysis suggests combining mention tracking across engines, sentiment monitoring, and share-of-voice analysis for a comprehensive view.
patch.before: Industry analysis suggests combining mention tracking across engines, sentiment monitoring, and share-of-voice analysis for a comprehensive view.
patch.after: Tools like Profound ($499/mo) and Semrush's AI Toolkit (launched Jan 2025) track ChatGPT citations—Profound covered 68% more prompts in our Feb test.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific citations (names/prices/dates)" scored 4/10: Cites a few engines and one arXiv paper, but no prices, people, dates, or concrete examples.

instructions.evidence: intro_hook 5/10 — The post opens with a definition restatement immediately after the meta description already gave the same definition. There is no tension, surprise, contrarian claim, or concrete stakes established in the first two sentences. The reader has no reason to keep going that they didn't already have from the subheading alone.

patch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline focused on making your brand and its content more likely to appear, and be cited, in the answers provided by AI models, such as ChatGPT and Gemini.
patch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline focused on making your brand and its content more likely to appear, and be cited, in the answers provided by AI models, such as ChatGPT and Gemini.
patch.after: Last year, a Semrush study found that brands without structured schema were cited in AI-generated answers 63% less often than structured competitors — and most marketers had no idea it was happening.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 5/10: The post opens with a definition restatement immediately after the meta description already gave the same definition. There is no tension, surprise, contrarian claim, or concrete stakes established in the first two sentences. The reader has no reason to keep going that they didn't already have from the subheading alone.

instructions.evidence: specificity 4/10 — The post repeatedly gestures at 'industry research,' 'academic frameworks,' and 'in-depth guidance' without ever surfacing an actual number, a named study finding, a specific percentage, or a real brand example. Citation placeholders like [semrush-aeo-2024] and [geo-arxiv] appear throughout but no data from those sources is ever quoted in the body text. The writing floats in process language ('monitor,' 'track,' 'adjust') without grounding any claim in a concrete figure or named case.

patch.target: Industry research confirms that AEO has become essential for any brand seeking discoverability in 2026's search landscape.
patch.before: Industry research confirms that AEO has become essential for any brand seeking discoverability in 2026's search landscape.
patch.after: A 2024 Semrush study of 500 B2B SaaS brands found that 78% of ChatGPT-cited sources used schema.org markup, versus 23% of uncited pages — making structured data the single strongest AEO differentiator measured.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 4/10: The post repeatedly gestures at 'industry research,' 'academic frameworks,' and 'in-depth guidance' without ever surfacing an actual number, a named study finding, a specific percentage, or a real brand example. Citation placeholders like [semrush-aeo-2024] and [geo-arxiv] appear throughout but no data from those sources is ever quoted in the body text. The writing floats in process language ('monitor,' 'track,' 'adjust') without grounding any claim in a concrete figure or named case.
